verdict.label: Worth checking
verdict.headline: Lagide After work (16:30 start)
verdict.reason: Lagide is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide makes the reef a bit less forgiving.

conversationalSummary.text: TODAY, I’d only head to Lagide for the after-work window (around 16:30) if the cams look much better than expected, because the wind angle’s not great and the swell period looks a bit flat for the reef shelves near high. Surfline and MEO Beachcam are the ones I’d check before leaving—if it’s still tuggy or underwhelming, I’ll skip it. If the light looks clean and you’re getting proper lip, it should still offer some workable lefts for your level.

wetsuit.reason: Warm water, warm air, and light wind make the 3/2 reasonable.

best.explanation: Lagide looks like a fun-sized window for Fun progression, Low confidence: 0.6-1.1 m, 96 kJ, 8 s period, consistent 74/100, and wind is the main risk.  A dependable left-hand reef that turns modest swell into real rides; this is best for clean, playful walls when the bigger beaches are too wild or too small; 6-9s at high tide is too flat and weak on the reef shelves.  Watch: wind angle is poor for this spot; 6-9s at high tide is too flat and weak on the reef shelves.
best.shortExplanation: Lagide is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide makes the reef a bit less forgiving.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot
6-9s at high tide is too flat and weak on the reef shelves

wetsuit.reason: Warm water, warm air, and light wind make the 3/2 reasonable.

ranked.explanation: Cerro looks like a fun-sized window for Worth checking, Low confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 79 kJ, 8 s period, consistent 74/100, and wind is not fully clear.  A sheltered beachbreak with gentle lefts and rights, somewhat similar to Cantinho but less consistent; this is best for manageable bodyboard waves when north-northwest swell reaches the bay with clean southerly wind.  Watch: 6-9s at high tide is usually too fat for exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia; Cerro may feel too soft without more push.
ranked.shortExplanation: Cerro is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide is part of the picture, but not the deciding factor.
warnings: 6-9s at high tide is usually too fat for exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia
Cerro may feel too soft without more push

photo.alt: Photo of Rey Cortiço surf spot

wetsuit.reason: Warm water, warm air, and light wind make the 3/2 reasonable.

ranked.explanation: Rey Cortiço looks like a fun-sized window for Rare window, Low confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 93 kJ, 8 s period, consistent 73/100, and wind is the main risk.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small.  Watch: wind angle is poor for this spot; 6-9s at high tide is usually too fat for Belgas; exposed north spot needs Lagide to be under 1 m.
ranked.shortExplanation: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide is fine, but Belgas can still work lower if the rest is better.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot

ranked.explanation: Almagreira looks like a fun-sized window for Rare window, Low confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 79 kJ, 16 s period, consistent 71/100, and wind is the main risk.  An exposed north-coast beach break with more raw swell than Baleal; this is best for small, clean north-coast days when protected spots look underpowered; 14s+ at high tide is usually still too heavy and walling on exposed beaches.  Watch: wind angle is poor for this spot; 14s+ at high tide is usually still too heavy and walling on exposed beaches; exposed north spot needs Lagide to be under 1 m.
